summary: filewisee ships a dropper exported as formatd that downloads and executes a binary from an anonymous file host
details: filewisee is advertised as a Node date-formatting utility, but dist/file.js exports a function `formatd` that fetches a binary from https://pixeldrain.com/u/byLkaSJR (an anonymous, mutable, opaque file-sharing host), creates ~/.drc, writes the response to ~/.drc/dr, chmods it 0o755, and spawns it with caller-supplied arguments. There is no version pinning, no hash check, and no signature verification. The dropper is co-listed in the package's index alongside genuine date helpers (formatDate, formatTime) and named to visually mimic them (`formatd`), increasing the chance a consumer invokes it via tutorial or auto-import. Invoking the package's advertised API runs attacker-controlled bytes on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
